messages.subject: [R-sig-genetics] Last chance: Project Quality and Management Best Practices
messages.subject_clean: Last chance: Project Quality and Management Best Practices
messages.body_plain:
Hello R-SIG-GENETICS members

Last chance - This seminar offers practical strategies to enhance the quality and management of your data analysis projects. Project Quality and Management for Statisticians and Data Scientists is a 2-day seminar livestreaming April 8 and 22 with Michiko Wolcott (Msight Analytics). Genetic data analysis projects, often conducted using R, frequently encounter non-statistical challenges like data quality issues, scoping uncertainty, and managing expectations. This seminar introduces best practices and methodologies in project management, delivery, and quality, offering a structured framework to assess and improve the robustness and transparency of your analytical workflows. By applying these principles, researchers can enhance the rigor and reproducibility of their genetic studies, ensuring more reliable and impactful results.

Sign up today (https://instats.org/seminar/project-quality-and-management-for-stati) to reserve your place before it starts, and feel free to circulate this with colleagues and students!


Best wishes

Michael Zyphur
Professor and Director
Instats | instats.org
	[[alternative HTML version deleted]]

messages.subject: [R-sig-genetics] Master R Programming for Statistics and Machine Learning
messages.subject_clean: Master R Programming for Statistics and Machine Learning
messages.body_plain:
Hello R-SIG-GENETICS members

Explore powerful R programming methods to elevate your statistical and machine learning research. R Programming for Statistics and Machine Learning is a 2-day seminar livestreaming May 11, led by Nikolay Oskolkov (Lund University). This seminar offers an integrated toolkit for contemporary statistical inference and machine learning in R, directly applicable to researchers analyzing high-dimensional genetic and genomic data. You will learn to implement techniques like penalised regression (LASSO), various dimensionality reduction methods (PCA, tSNE, UMAP), and advanced clustering algorithms, all crucial for identifying patterns and making robust inferences in complex genetic datasets. Mastering these R programming patterns will enhance your ability to design, implement, and report sophisticated analyses, leading to more reproducible and impactful research in genetic data analysis.

Sign up today (https://instats.org/seminar/r-programming-for-statistics-and-machine) to secure your spot, and please share this opportunity with colleagues and students who might benefit!


Best wishes

Michael Zyphur
Professor and Director
Instats | instats.org
	[[alternative HTML version deleted]]

messages.subject: [R-sig-genetics] Single Cell Data Analysis with R and Python
messages.subject_clean: Single Cell Data Analysis with R and Python
messages.body_plain:
Hello R-SIG-GENETICS members

Discover how to leverage advanced techniques for interpreting single-cell data in your research. Single Cell Data Analysis 2.0 led by Nikolay Oskolkov (Lund University) is now available as an on-demand streaming seminar. This seminar offers advanced computational tools and methodologies for single-cell data analysis, crucial for dissecting cellular heterogeneity in genetic research. Researchers in genetics can apply these techniques, including preprocessing, clustering, and differential expression, to uncover nuanced genetic variations and expression patterns within diverse cell populations using R and Python. Mastering this comprehensive pipeline, from raw data handling to biological interpretation, will empower your genetic data analysis with more accurate and insightful single-cell resolution.

Access this seminar (https://instats.org/seminar/single-cell-data-analysis-20-1) on demand at your own pace, and please pass this to colleagues and students who may find it useful!


Best wishes

Michael Zyphur
Professor and Director
Instats | instats.org

messages.subject: [R-sig-genetics] Longitudinal Analysis in Biostatistics - Mastering Longitudinal Analysis
messages.subject_clean: Longitudinal Analysis in Biostatistics - Mastering Longitudinal Analysis
messages.body_plain:
Hello R-SIG-GENETICS members

Understanding complex temporal data is essential for robust research across many scientific disciplines. Longitudinal Analysis in Biostatistics led by Md Tuhin Sheikh (Biostatistics, College of Health Solutions, Arizona State University) is now available as an on-demand streaming seminar. This seminar provides a comprehensive overview of longitudinal data analysis, focusing on advanced modeling frameworks, strategies for handling missing data, and multilevel modeling. Researchers in genetic data analysis often encounter repeated observations over time, where understanding temporal dynamics and handling complex data structures is crucial. Participants will gain hands-on experience with these techniques in R, enabling them to robustly analyze genetic datasets, interpret complex temporal relationships, and advance their own research.

Access this seminar (https://instats.org/seminar/longitudinal-analysis-in-biostatistics) on demand at your own pace, and please pass this to colleagues and students who may find it useful!


Best wishes

Michael Zyphur
Professor and Director
Instats | instats.org

messages.subject: [R-sig-genetics] Last chance: Deep Learning for Complex Datasets with AI
messages.subject_clean: Last chance: Deep Learning for Complex Datasets with AI
messages.body_plain:
Hello R-SIG-GENETICS members

Last chance - This intensive workshop offers practical skills to advance your data analysis capabilities. Deep Learning for Complex Datasets with AI 3.0 is a 2-day seminar livestreaming April 15 with Nikolay Oskolkov (Lund University). Deep learning techniques, including those enhanced by Large Language Models like ChatGPT, are crucial for analyzing complex genetic and multi-omics datasets, enabling researchers to uncover intricate patterns and hidden structures. This seminar provides hands-on experience with R, Python, TensorFlow, and Keras, guided by ChatGPT, to build and apply neural network architectures. Participants will develop practical skills to integrate diverse biological datasets, advance their genetic data analysis, and apply cutting-edge AI methods to their own research projects in areas like single-cell biology and personalized medicine.

Sign up today (https://instats.org/seminar/deep-learning-for-complex-datasets-with-4) to register now, and we'd appreciate you sharing this with colleagues and students who might benefit!


Best wishes

Michael Zyphur
Professor and Director
Instats | instats.org
	[[alternative HTML version deleted]]
